summary: path-extend fetches and eval()s remote JavaScript on require
details: On module load, path.js invokes an IIFE that calls loadStringData(), which fetches a base64-decoded URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/XTTBX) and passes the JSON `content` field directly to eval(). A second loader (loadTokenData) is wired to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/P0CND with the same fetch→eval pattern. The README claims the package is `an exact copy of the NodeJS 'path' module`, providing a lure to entice installers into requiring it. jsonkeeper.com is a mutable third-party paste host, so the executed code is attacker-controlled and can change at any time without a package update. Any project that requires path-extend executes whatever JavaScript the operator places at those URLs with the full privileges of the host process — credential theft, file system access, persistence, lateral movement, or any subsequent payload are all possible.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
